metadata_details.purpose: Parser for generic logs migrated from samples-main
metadata_details.dependency_summary: Requires ingestion of generic logs in appropriate format
metadata_details.author: Joel Mora

analysis.semantic_summary: This parser processes Okta identity and access management logs, transforming raw Okta JSON events into standardized OCSF format. It captures authentication events, user activities, geographical context, and device information with detailed field mapping for comprehensive security monitoring and audit trails.
optimization_opportunities: Group related field transformations into batch operations
Implement caching for repeated geographical lookups
Add error handling for malformed JSON
analysis.lua_code:
-- SentinelOne Parser: okta_logs-latest 
-- OCSF Class: Access Activity (3006)
-- Performance Level: High-volume optimized
-- Generated: 2025-10-13T11:10:53.635439

-- Pre-declare locals for performance
local type = type
local pairs = pairs
local os_time = os.time
local string_format = string.format
local table_insert = table.insert

-- Validation helper functions
local function validate_string(value)
    return type(value) == "string" and #value > 0
end

local function validate_table(value)
    return type(value) == "table" and next(value) ~= nil
end

-- Safe table accessor to handle nested paths
local function safe_get(tbl, path)
    if not tbl then return nil end
    local current = tbl
    for segment in path:gmatch("[^%.]+") do
        if type(current) ~= "table" then return nil end
        current = current[segment]
    end
    return current
end

-- Safe table setter with path creation
local function safe_set(tbl, path, value)
    if not tbl or not path then return false end
    local current = tbl
    local segments = {}
    for segment in path:gmatch("[^%.]+") do
        table_insert(segments, segment)
    end
    
    for i=1, #segments-1 do
        local key = segments[i]
        current[key] = current[key] or {}
        current = current[key]
    end
    current[segments[#segments]] = value
    return true
end

function transform(record)
    -- Input validation with detailed error messages
    if not validate_table(record) then
        return nil, "Invalid input record: expected table"
    end

    -- Initialize OCSF-compliant output with static fields
    local output = {
        metadata = {
            product = {
                vendor_name = "Okta",
                name = "Okta",
                version = "1.0.0-rc.2"
            }
        },
        class_uid = 3006,
        class_name = "Access Activity", 
        category_uid = 3,
        category_name = "Audit Activity",
        type_uid = 300699,
        severity_id = 99,
        activity_id = 99,
        dataSource = {
            category = "security"
        }
    }

    -- Field mapping with error handling
    local field_mappings = {
        -- Actor information
        {"actor.alternateId", "actor.user.email_addr"},
        {"actor.displayName", "actor.user.name"},
        {"actor.id", "actor.user.uid"},
        {"actor.type", "actor.user.type"},
        
        -- Authentication context
        {"authenticationContext.externalSession", "actor.session.uid"},
        {"authenticationContext.issuer.id", "actor.idp.uid"},
        {"authenticationContext.issuer.type", "actor.idp.name"},
        
        -- Device information
        {"client.ipAddress", "device.ip"},
        {"client.device", "device.name"},
        {"client.userAgent.browser", "device.browser"},
        {"client.userAgent.os", "device.os.name"},
        
        -- Location data
        {"client.geographicalContext.city", "device.location.city"},
        {"client.geographicalContext.country", "device.location.country"},
        {"client.geographicalContext.geolocation.lat", "device.location.geo_coordinates.latitude"},
        {"client.geographicalContext.geolocation.lon", "device.location.geo_coordinates.longitude"}
    }

    -- Batch process field mappings
    local errors = {}
    for _, mapping in ipairs(field_mappings) do
        local source, target = mapping[1], mapping[2]
        local value = safe_get(record, source)
        if value then
            safe_set(output, target, value)
        end
    end

    -- Handle required fields
    output.time = safe_get(record, "published") or (os_time() * 1000)
    output.activity_name = safe_get(record, "eventType")
    output.message = safe_get(record, "displayMessage")
    output.status = safe_get(record, "outcome.result")
    output.status_detail = safe_get(record, "outcome.reason")
    
    -- Validation of critical fields
    if not output.activity_name then
        table_insert(errors, "Missing required field: activity_name")
    end

    -- Return with any validation errors
    if #errors > 0 then
        return output, string_format("Validation warnings: %s", table.concat(errors, "; "))
    end

    return output
end
